name: Transmission Lines
one_liner: A bundle of glowing fiber-optic cables receding into a dark vanishing point, with bright light pulses racing down each glass core at near-light speed -- uncompressed video carried as light in glass.
visual_description: Looking down a bundle of thin fiber-optic strands as they sweep across a near-black field and converge toward a deep vanishing point with atmospheric haze. Each strand is a faintly lit glass tube; inside it, discrete bright dashes of light -- the light pulses carrying data -- race along the core at high speed, leaving short streaking trails and an internal-reflection shimmer that bounces along the glass. The bundle is mostly cyan (the hero fiber color), with a few strands in phosphor-green, magenta, and amber to read as separated essence channels. Near strands in the foreground are thick, bright, and bokeh-soft (shallow depth of field); far strands dim into the haze. The pulses are quantized -- you can count them -- and they all pulse a touch brighter on a steady PTP beat. Volumetric glow blooms each pulse into a halo; the brightest pulses occasionally throw a tiny lens streak. It reads instantly as light traveling through glass: video over fiber at the speed of light.
technique: Single self-contained GLSL TOP generator (no input). The shader lays out N analytic fiber strands as smooth curves across the frame converging toward a vanishing point, computes per-pixel distance to the nearest strand (a thin glowing tube with an internal-reflection gradient), and then renders traveling light pulses as bright Gaussian dashes whose position along each strand advances with time and wraps (endless travel). Depth is faked by a per-strand depth value driving width, brightness, haze, and a cheap depth-of-field blur weight. A PTP beat brightens all pulses in lockstep. Everything is cheap per-pixel trig + a bounded strand loop. Bloom finish carries the volumetric glow. No feedback, no input -- drops in anywhere.

network_plan.name: glsl_fibers
network_plan.role: The whole piece. Generator (no input): N fiber strands converging to a vanishing point, glass-tube glow with internal-reflection gradient, traveling light pulses, depth haze + fake DOF, PTP beat. Generator MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res; NEVER uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Three vec4 uniforms via the vec SEQUENCE (declared manually). Place nodeX 400, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom
network_plan.role: Bloom + finish. Input0 = glsl_fibers. Bright-pass + bounded blur added back for volumetric pulse glow; a faint scanline + grain. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Place nodeX 800, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: out1
network_plan.role: Manifest output_op / capture target, named out1. Viewer flagged. Place nodeX 1200, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_fibers_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_fibers. See glsl[0]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_fibers_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_fibers.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_bloom. See glsl[1]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_bloom_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_bloom.

network_plan.name: annotate_fibers
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over the fiber generator (glsl_fibers). Mode annotate, Opacity 0.3.

params.Bodytext: One generator GLSL TOP. N analytic strands converge to a vanishing point; per pixel it finds the nearest strand and draws a glowing glass tube with an internal-reflection gradient. Traveling light pulses are bright Gaussian dashes whose position advances with time and wraps -- endless light in glass. A per-strand depth drives width, brightness, haze, and a fake depth-of-field weight for real Z-space.

network_plan.name: annotate_finish
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over bloom + out (glsl_bloom, out1). Mode annotate.

params.Bodytext: Bloom blows each white-hot pulse into a colored halo for the volumetric light-in-glass look; a faint scanline + grain keeps the dark gradient clean at booth scale. Terminates in out1.

exposed_params.name: Strands
exposed_params.label: Strand Count
exposed_params.drives: uFiber.x -- number of fiber strands in the bundle. More = a denser, more premium fiber bundle. Note: each strand runs a bounded inner sample/pulse loop, so cost scales with count; cap 28.

exposed_params.name: Bundlewidth
exposed_params.label: Bundle Spread
exposed_params.drives: uFiber.y -- vertical spread of the strands near the camera before they converge to the vanishing point. Wider = a fanned bundle; tight = a focused cable.

exposed_params.name: Vanishdepth
exposed_params.label: Vanishing Depth
exposed_params.drives: uFiber.z -- how strongly the strands recede toward the vanishing point (perspective depth). Higher = a deeper Z-space tunnel of fiber.

exposed_params.name: Glassglow
exposed_params.label: Glass Glow
exposed_params.drives: uFiber.w -- strength of the internal-reflection shimmer gradient along each glass tube. The light-bouncing-in-glass look.

exposed_params.name: Pulsespeed
exposed_params.label: Pulse Speed
exposed_params.drives: uPulse.x and the time term uPulse.w -- how fast the light pulses race down the strands. 0 freezes them (and drops time-dependence); higher = near-light-speed streaks.

exposed_params.name: Pulsedensity
exposed_params.label: Pulse Density
exposed_params.drives: uPulse.y -- how many light pulses travel down each strand at once (1-6). More = a busier data stream.

exposed_params.name: Pulselength
exposed_params.label: Pulse Length
exposed_params.drives: uPulse.z -- the length of each Gaussian light dash. Short = crisp countable packets; long = streaking light trails.

exposed_params.name: Haze
exposed_params.label: Atmospheric Haze
exposed_params.drives: uLook.x -- volumetric haze building toward the vanishing point. Adds depth so distant strands dim out into fog.

exposed_params.name: Dof
exposed_params.label: Depth of Field
exposed_params.drives: uLook.y -- fake depth-of-field weight: near strands stay sharp, far ones soften (bokeh-glow). Adds the shallow-DOF premium look.

exposed_params.name: Ptprate
exposed_params.label: PTP Beat Rate
exposed_params.drives: uLook.z -- frequency of the PTP sync pulse all light pulses breathe brighter to in lockstep.

exposed_params.name: Bloom
exposed_params.label: Bloom
exposed_params.drives: uBloom.x -- intensity of the volumetric bloom glow added over the pulses. The booth-scale wow knob.

palette.ratio: ~85% dark charcoal + dim glass tubes, ~15% saturated pulse/glow; cyan dominant (fiber hero color), green/magenta/amber as a few essence strands.

conference_fit: The most direct visualization of the medium itself -- light pulses traveling through fiber-optic glass, which is literally how uncompressed video moves over IP. Cyan-dominant (the fiber-optic-coded hero color) on a dark premium stage, with deep Z-space, atmospheric haze, and shallow DOF that read as a high-end render at 10+ feet. The traveling pulses are quantized and countable (close-inspection payoff) and snap to a PTP beat (ST2110 timing). A clean, self-contained generator -- ideal as a looping backdrop while the denser packet/crosspoint pieces anchor the foreground.

prompt.one_liner: A self-contained fiber-optic bundle generator: glowing glass strands receding to a vanishing point with bright light pulses racing down each core (cyan hero + green/magenta/amber essence channels), depth haze, fake DOF, a PTP beat, and a volumetric bloom finish.
prompt.evolve: Raise Strand Count + Pulse Density for a busier bundle; raise Pulse Speed for near-light-speed streaks (0 freezes); shorten Pulse Length for crisp countable packets or lengthen for light trails. Raise Atmospheric Haze + Depth of Field for a deeper, dreamier render. Sweep PTP Beat for the sync breath. Feed out1 into the kaleidoscope specimen for a radial fiber mandala, or composite under the packet-fabric swarm. Drive Pulse Speed from audio for a reactive loop. To extend: add chromatic dispersion (split the pulse into RGB along the strand) at the brightest pulses.

build_notes:
GENERATOR CORRECTNESS: glsl_fibers has NO input -- MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res (res=(1/w,1/h,w,h)); uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator is a compile error. outputresolution MUST be 'custom' + resolutionw/h or it renders 1x1. glsl_bloom HAS an input so sTD2DInputs[0] + uTDOutputInfo.res are valid.

LOOP BOUNDS (GPU crash safety): the fiber shader has THREE nested constant-bounded loops -- outer strands (MAXS=28), an inner curve-projection sample (STEPS=24), and a per-strand pulse loop (k<6). All are bounded by CONSTANT literals (the param-driven counts only EARLY-OUT via break against the constant bound) so no unbounded GPU loop. COST: with all caps maxed this is 28*(24+6) ~= 840 iterations per pixel at 1280x720 -- the heaviest of the transmission set. GATE PERFORMANCE: baseline get_project_performance before, re-check after; if fps drops below ~90% of target, lower the default Strand Count or reduce STEPS to 16. The default 14 strands is comfortable. This is a true generator (not a heavy-fBm-then-cheap-downstream case) so it recomputes per frame -- acceptable at these caps, but it is the one piece to watch.

UNIFORMS: glslTOP declares uniforms MANUALLY. vec parameter SEQUENCE (op.seq.vec.numBlocks=N, vecNname, vecNtype='vec4', vecNvaluex..w expressions), NOT op.par.vec.

COOK DEMAND: time-dependent via absTime in uPulse.w and uLook.w. Cooks every frame only when out1 demanded; add a temp Execute DAT OUTSIDE the COMP in the sandbox; verify with two captures separated by a real sleep. Pulse Speed=0 freezes the pulses but uLook.w still references absTime (PTP beat), so the TOP keeps cooking -- correct.

DOCKED DATS: glsl_fibers + glsl_bloom each dock pixel+compute+info; HUG all six under their hosts in the SAME execute_python that creates each op, then get_network_layout. LF/space-indented shader source for a clean YAML literal block.

TDN ROUND-TRIP: all 11 params single-component (Int/Float) -> default->value seeding works on re-import (verified v6.0.26). Re-import into a throwaway COMP, confirm a couple .vals + out1 renders, destroy it. operator_count = all ops incl docked DATs (annotations not counted): ~9.
